what_it_does: This file orchestrates a persisted vector index for a store: it builds/refreshes embeddings incrementally by content hash, queries the index by cosine similarity (reusing existing fabric and retrieve nodes), reports index staleness without embedding, and provides convenience pass-throughs to the underlying store's vector accessors.
observations: Builds embeddings only for new or changed items using a blake2b content hash; unchanged items are skipped.
On embedder failure it writes no vectors, appends a durable warning event, returns degraded=True, and does not crash.
Supports named projection spaces via composed vec:// addresses so the same source item can have independent embeddings per space.
Reuses fabric.transport.openai_embeddings_transport, fabric.client.complete_embeddings, and nodes.retrieve rather than reimplementing embedding or cosine.
Includes a numpy matrix fast path for per-space queries with a fallback to the existing retrieve node.
query_index can return an honest note distinguishing an empty index from no strong matches.
index_staleness reads content hashes only, touching no network.
Convenience functions index_addresses and get_vector simply delegate to the store.
standouts: The degrade-with-warning contract: embed failure produces a durable warning event and an honest empty/partial index rather than fabricated vectors.
The space_matrix fast path plus retrieve fallback attempts to speed up large-space queries while preserving fail-loud dim checks.
index_staleness lets callers interrogate freshness before trusting rankings, without triggering a rebuild or network call.
conventions: Heavy module-level docstring stating design rationale, constraints, and cross-references.
Additive parameter defaults: space=None preserves legacy bare-address behavior; new projection-space behavior is opt-in.
Fail-loud by reuse: dim and cosine checks are delegated to existing fabric/retrieve code.
Hermetic seam: _default_embed is injectable via embed_fn for deterministic tests.
Best-effort event logging for degradation, swallowing append_event failures.
concerns: Broad Exception catches in build_index and query_index may hide programming errors or store contract violations.
query_index's matrix path silently falls back to retrieve on any error with no logging of the edge condition.
index_staleness may perform O(N) store.get_vector calls for changed/missing checks.
with_note empty-index hint hardcodes space names and port :8007, which can drift from actual configuration.
Several store methods (space_address, space_matrix, index_corpus, index_addresses, put_vector, get_vector, append_event, ALL_SPACES) are assumed but not defined in this file.
_default_embed and fabric imports are deferred inside functions, making static analysis harder.
notes: A fail-loud, incrementally-built vector index orchestrator with honest degradation reporting and optional projection spaces.
questions: What is the exact contract of the store object (methods like space_address, space_matrix, index_corpus, index_addresses, put_vector, get_vector, append_event, ALL_SPACES)?
How does fabric.config.resolve_emb_layer behave for __default__, None, and named layers?
What is the precise shape of records passed to query_index and how is it produced?
Does nodes.retrieve raise ValueError on dimension mismatch as assumed, and what does it return for an empty corpus?
How are durable warning events consumed or surfaced to operators?
Is the numpy matrix path numerically equivalent to retrieve.run for all vector lengths and tie cases?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds/refreshes, queries, and checks staleness of a persisted vector index while reusing existing fabric and retrieve nodes.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud correctness through reuse rather than reimplementation.
Honest degradation: no fabricated vectors or silent fallbacks.
Additive feature evolution: projection spaces without breaking default behavior.
Separation of concerns: store handles bytes/persistence; this module handles model-facing orchestration.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make semantic retrieval durable, observable, and incrementally maintainable across multiple conceptual projection spaces, while remaining honest when the embedding infrastructure is unavailable.
novelty: The combination of content-hash incremental embedding, a read-only staleness interrogation, and space-keyed projection addresses within a single persisted substrate, plus explicit degrade-with-warning behavior.
contribution: It contributes a persisted vector index orchestration layer that incrementalizes embedding work via content hashes, surfaces embedder unavailability through durable warnings, supports opt-in projection spaces, and lets callers check index freshness before trusting query results.
summary_for_embedding: store/vector_index.py orchestrates building, incrementally refreshing, querying, and staleness-checking a persisted vector index over addressed text items using content hashes and reused embedding/retrieval fabric.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to provide a durable, fail-loud, incrementally-maintained semantic retrieval layer with honest degradation reporting and support for named projection spaces.

symbols.does: Computes a stable blake2b content hash for an addressed item's text.
symbols.description: Returns a UTF-8 blake2b digest prefixed with 'b2:', used as the incremental key to decide whether an item needs re-embedding. It mirrors the store's own CAS hashing discipline.

symbols.does: Performs the actual fabric embedding call used by the build path.
symbols.description: Wraps fabric.client.complete_embeddings using the same transport path as nodes/embed.py. Separated as an injectable default so tests can substitute a deterministic stub. Raises on endpoint failure, which build_index treats as a degrade signal.

symbols.does: Builds or refreshes the persisted vector index over a corpus incrementally.
symbols.description: Computes content hashes, skips unchanged items, embeds the changed batch in one round-trip, and persists vectors via store.put_vector keyed by space-composed addresses. On embed failure it writes nothing, emits a durable warning, and returns degraded=True. Supports named projection spaces and an injectable embed_fn.

symbols.does: Queries the persisted index with a vector and returns top-k nearest addresses.
symbols.description: Uses a per-space numpy matrix fast path when available, otherwise falls back to nodes.retrieve for fail-loud cosine ranking. Can return an honest note when the index is empty or the top match is weak. Filters by projection space and embedding layer.

symbols.does: Read-only staleness check comparing corpus content hashes to persisted entries.
symbols.description: Embeds nothing and touches no network. Reports missing, changed, and extra addresses for a given space, enabling callers to decide whether to rebuild before trusting rankings. Fail-loud on malformed corpus items.

symbols.does: Convenience pass-through returning sorted indexed addresses.
symbols.description: Delegates to store.index_addresses, optionally filtered by projection space or all spaces.

symbols.does: Convenience pass-through returning a persisted vector record.
symbols.description: Delegates to store.get_vector for the full record including vector, content_hash, dim, model, and timestamp.
